title: Embedder-mismatch guard bypassed when snapshot embedderSlug is null

findingDetails.evidence: Guard is `manifest.embedderSlug !== null && manifest.embedderSlug !== liveEmbedderSlug`. vecTables is set independently of slug at create (2311: vecIncluded ? ['vec_memories','vec_metadata'] : []), so a snapshot can carry embedderSlug=null while shouldRestoreVec=true (2626). When null, the guard short-circuits and the vec shard is copied over the live shard (2695) with no embedder/dimension compatibility check.
findingDetails.why: If shard naming ever fails the deriveEmbedderSlug regex (context-vectors__<slug>.sqlite), slug is null for both create and live, the guard is permanently dead, and a vec shard with a different embedding model/dimension is restored silently over the live shard — corrupting similarity search. The guard exists specifically to prevent this.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat a null manifest.embedderSlug as untrusted when shouldRestoreVec is true: refuse with CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_EMBEDDER_MISMATCH (or compare embedding dimensions via run_vector_shard_integrity_probe_at_path) rather than bypassing the check.

title: v2 restore reports manifest memoryCount, not actual swapped-in row count

findingDetails.evidence: `result.restored = manifest.memoryCount` where memoryCount was captured from the live DB after VACUUM at create (2313: countTableRows(database,'memory_index')). The adjacent workingMemoryRestored uses an actual post-swap count (2756: countTableRows(newDb,'working_memory')).
findingDetails.why: A count drift (cross-process write between VACUUM and the count read) or a truncated-but-openable snapshot makes restore report a confident 'restored N memories' that does not match what is actually present, undermining post-restore verification.
findingDetails.recommendation: Set result.restored from an actual countTableRows(newDb,'memory_index') after the swap, and optionally warn when it diverges from manifest.memoryCount.

title: v2 catalog merge resurrects checkpoints deleted after snapshot creation

findingDetails.evidence: VACUUM main INTO (2274) copies the entire checkpoints table into snapshot-main (the 'skip' manifest only affects v1 row loops, not the file copy). mergeCheckpointCatalogRows (964-998) inserts live rows missing by name but never removes rows present only in the snapshot. Checkpoints deleted between create and restore (deleteCheckpoint removes the dir at 3257) thus reappear as catalog rows with no snapshot dir.
findingDetails.why: Restore does not faithfully reproduce the catalog: it yields snapshot∪live, leaving phantom rows that appear in listCheckpoints and fail loadCheckpointV2Manifest on a restore attempt. Self-limited by MAX_CHECKPOINTS pruning, hence minor.
findingDetails.recommendation: After merge, reconcile the catalog against on-disk snapshot dirs (drop v2 rows whose snapshot_path is missing), or rebuild the post-restore catalog purely from liveCheckpointCatalogRows instead of unioning with the snapshot's stale catalog.
